extra.message: User input is formatted directly into a SQL string via EXECUTE_QUERY_FMT.format(user_input) and passed to cursor.execute(). This allows SQL injection — an attacker can break out of the INSERT statement to execute arbitrary SQL.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User input is formatted directly into a SQL string via EXECUTEMANY_QUERY_FMT.format(user_input) and passed to cursor.executemany(). This allows SQL injection through the format-string interpolation of the table/column portion of the query.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User input is formatted directly into a SQL script string via EXECUTESCRIPT_QUERY_FMT.format(user_input) and passed to cursor.executescript(). executescript() executes multiple SQL statements, making this particularly dangerous — an attacker can inject arbitrary SQL statements.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed directly to os.system(command). os.system() passes the command to the shell, allowing an attacker to inject shell metacharacters (e.g., ; && |) to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed directly to subprocess.Popen() with shell=True. With shell=True, the command string is passed to the shell interpreter, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed directly to exec(). exec() executes arbitrary Python code, allowing a remote attacker to run any Python code in the context of the server process.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed directly to eval(). eval() evaluates arbitrary Python expressions, allowing a remote attacker to execute arbitrary Python code.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed directly to compile(). compile() compiles source code into a code object that can then be exec'd. Passing untrusted input enables code injection.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input is deserialized with pickle.load(). Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows an attacker to execute arbitrary code by crafting a malicious pickle payload (e.g., using __reduce__ to invoke os.system).

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User-controlled input is deserialized with pickle.loads(). Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows an attacker to execute arbitrary code by crafting a malicious pickle payload.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed to yaml.load() with yaml.UnsafeLoader. UnsafeLoader can deserialize arbitrary Python objects, enabling remote code execution via crafted YAML payloads (e.g., using !!python/object/apply: to invoke os.system).

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User-controlled input is passed to yaml.load_all() with yaml.UnsafeLoader. UnsafeLoader deserializes all YAML documents with full Python object construction, enabling remote code execution via crafted YAML payloads.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: The X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0', disabling browser-level XSS filtering. While modern browsers no longer rely on this header, explicitly disabling it removes a defense-in-depth layer.

extra.message: Cache-Control header is set to 'public' without 'no-store' or 'no-cache' directives. Sensitive responses may be cached by intermediate proxies, leading to information disclosure.

extra.message: Strict-Transport-Security header is set with max-age=0, effectively disabling HSTS. This means browsers will not enforce HTTPS for this site, enabling downgrade attacks.

extra.message: The regex pattern r'((a)+)+' is vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS). When applied to user-controlled input containing a long string of 'a' characters followed by a non-matching character, the regex engine can take exponential time, causing denial of service. All functions in this module apply this pattern to user input.

cwe: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
